name: Muon anomalous magnetic moment a_mu (Fermilab/BNL world average)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the world-average muon anomalous magnetic moment a_mu = (g_mu - 2)/2 = 116592059(22) x 10^-11 (Fermilab Muon g-2 final 2025 result combined with BNL E821) and account for the discrepancy with at least one of the two independent SM theory determinations: (a) the data-driven Theory Initiative White Paper 2020 SM, with which the world average sits ~5 sigma high, or (b) the lattice-QCD-based SM (BMW 2020 / RBC-UKQCD / Mainz / converging) with which the world average sits ~1-2 sigma high. The framework must commit to a position: BSM contribution to the muon dipole, hadronic-vacuum-polarization systematic, or a combination - and accept the consequences elsewhere (e.g. on the muon EDM, on hadronic-vacuum-polarization-dependent observables like the running of alpha, on lepton-flavor universality).

regime.description: Muon-scale physics. a_mu integrates loop momenta from m_mu up to where new physics decouples; sensitive to virtual contributions of any particle that couples directly or indirectly to the muon.

current_bound.value: a_mu^exp = 116592059(22) x 10^-11 (Fermilab Muon g-2 Run-1+2+3 combined with BNL E821, 2023); SM (WP2020 data-driven) = 116591810(43) x 10^-11; SM (BMW lattice 2020) = 116591954(55) x 10^-11; world-average sigma vs SM is 5.1 (data-driven) / 1.8 (lattice).
current_bound.experiment: FNAL Muon g-2 Run-1+2+3 combined with BNL E821 (Aguillard et al. 2023)
current_bound.reference: Aguillard et al. (FNAL Muon g-2), 'Measurement of the Positive Muon Anomalous Magnetic Moment to 0.20 ppm', PRL 131, 161802 (2023), arXiv:2308.06230

what_it_excludes: Frameworks that predict no new contribution to the muon dipole are forced to pick a side in the lattice-vs-data-driven HVP dispute, and must explain the CMD-3 vs KLOE/BaBar discrepancy in e+e- -> pi+pi- cross sections. Frameworks that predict significant new contributions to a_mu (e.g. SUSY with light smuons + binos at moderate tan(beta), TeV-scale leptoquarks, two-Higgs-doublet models with tau-philic couplings) are constrained simultaneously by the muon EDM (muon_edm_bnl), by the electron g-2 (electron_g_minus_2_precision), by direct LHC searches, and by lepton-flavor universality (R(K), R(D), tau decays). Naive correlated explanations are increasingly tightly bracketed; the surviving parameter space is narrow.

parameterization.name: SM decomposition a_mu^SM = a_mu^QED + a_mu^EW + a_mu^HVP + a_mu^HLbL plus BSM additions captured in SMEFT as muon-flavor entries of the dipole operators C_eB^(22), C_eW^(22) (combined into C_e gamma^(22) after EWSB).
parameterization.reference: Aoyama et al., Phys. Rep. 887, 1 (2020) [Theory Initiative White Paper 2020], arXiv:2006.04822; Borsanyi et al. (BMW), Nature 593, 51 (2021), arXiv:2002.12347; CMD-3, arXiv:2302.08834; Crivellin, Hoferichter, Schmidt-Wellenburg, PRD 98, 113002 (2018), arXiv:1807.11484.
parameterization.summary: The dominant SM uncertainty is the leading-order hadronic vacuum polarization a_mu^HVP. Two SM determinations exist: data-driven dispersive integral of e+e- -> hadrons (WP2020) and lattice-QCD computation (BMW and others). They disagree at ~2-2.5 sigma in their HVP value, and the world-average a_mu^exp is high relative to the data-driven SM by ~5 sigma but only ~1-2 sigma high relative to the lattice SM. Within the data-driven approach, CMD-3's pi+pi- cross-section measurement disagrees with KLOE / BaBar / SND at multi-sigma in dominant energy bins.

procedural.predicted_observable: a_mu directly. BSM contribution Delta a_mu^BSM = (m_mu v / sqrt(2)) Re(C_e gamma^(22)) / Lambda^2 in SMEFT; correlated to Im(C_e gamma^(22)) which sets the muon EDM.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Required Delta a_mu^BSM depends on which SM is adopted: ~250 x 10^-11 (vs WP2020 data-driven) or ~110 x 10^-11 (vs BMW lattice). For new heavy mediators with chiral enhancement, the corresponding NP scale is Lambda ~ 1-3 TeV with O(1) couplings; without chiral enhancement it can be sub-TeV.
framework_inputs: Predicted Delta a_mu^BSM in the framework's parameter space.

Position on the data-driven vs lattice HVP dispute: does the framework alter low-energy hadronic vacuum polarization (e.g. via modified e+e- -> hadrons couplings), or does it leave the SM HVP untouched and add a clean BSM dipole contribution?
Correlated predictions for: muon EDM (Im(C_e gamma)), the running of alpha at high energy (probed at LEP / future e+e-), and any companion observable in flavor or precision physics.
procedural.notes: The status of a_mu as a discovery channel is currently in flux: the lattice-vs-data-driven HVP dispute has to settle before a clean SM number can be quoted. In the meantime, candidate frameworks must be evaluated against both SM determinations and against the auxiliary constraints. See data/muon_g_minus_2.json for the historical tension trajectory and full list of measurements and SM evaluations.

primary_references.citation: Aguillard et al. (FNAL Muon g-2 Collaboration), 'Measurement of the Positive Muon Anomalous Magnetic Moment to 0.20 ppm', PRL 131, 161802 (2023)
Aoyama et al. (Theory Initiative White Paper 2020), 'The anomalous magnetic moment of the muon in the Standard Model', Phys. Rep. 887, 1-166 (2020)
Borsanyi et al. (BMW), 'Leading hadronic contribution to the muon magnetic moment from lattice QCD', Nature 593, 51 (2021)
CMD-3 Collaboration, 'Measurement of the e+e- -> pi+pi- cross section from threshold to 1.2 GeV with the CMD-3 detector', arXiv:2302.08834 (2023)
